---
name: Technical review feedback
about: Calm feedback on public framing, demo plan, boundaries, or review questions
title: "Review feedback: "
labels: review-feedback
assignees: ""
---

## What did you review?

- [ ] README
- [ ] Public current status
- [ ] Public boundaries
- [ ] Public Body Shadow checks
- [ ] Public engineering contracts
- [ ] Public memory/residue boundaries
- [ ] First public demo plan
- [ ] Demo spec
- [ ] Review questions
- [ ] Other:

## Main feedback

Please describe one concrete point.

Examples:

- a term was unclear;
- a boundary sounded too strong or too weak;
- Body Shadow was understandable / not understandable;
- static/shadow evidence sounded too close to live behavior;
- memory/residue language sounded like active authority;
- LLM speech sounded like the source of choice;
- the proposed artifact needs a clearer comparison.

## Why it matters

Explain whether this affects:

- clarity;
- safety of public claims;
- reproducibility;
- technical review;
- boundary wording;
- artifact usefulness.

## Suggested change

If possible, suggest a small wording or structure change.

Please keep the suggestion public-safe and narrow.

## Boundary note

Please do not ask for or include:

- private workbench files;
- raw logs;
- local paths;
- unpublished notes;
- private implementation details;
- internal report filenames;
- protected hashes;
- private owner paths;
- private schema details;
- hidden channels;
- private runtime traces.

Please also avoid treating static/shadow evidence as live behavior, actual selected results, memory authority, speech authority, consciousness, life, or a finished autonomous system.
